name: Mousey Alexander
abstract: Elmer "Mousey" Alexander (June 19, 1922 – October 9, 1988) was an American jazz drummer.

concerts.label: Midnight Performance: Dody Goodman in "Dody in Dixieland"
Clark Terry and His Big Band

works.t: Blues All Day, Blues All Night

about_quotes.speaker: Derek Smith
about_quotes.evidence: So my friend, he’s dead now, the drummer Mousey Alexander, a good friend of mine
about_quotes.doc_title: Derek Smith interviewed by Monk Rowe and Michael Woods, Majesty of the Seas, May 29, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Eddie Sauter
about_quotes.evidence: I did get to use it in that festival with the German band I had... Alexander's big mouth was always involved with Ray McKinley too... I remember before we went on that night, he took Bill and I into his little cubicle

about_quotes.speaker: Cliff Leeman
about_quotes.evidence: Let's see, there's myself on drums, and Duke Hanna, and Mousey Alexander, Bobby Rosengarden - that's a motley crew!

about_quotes.speaker: Buck Clayton
about_quotes.evidence: That was my very, very first band that I played in and I played first trumpet... Mousey Alexander the drummer here

about_quotes.speaker: George Masso
about_quotes.evidence: Mousey Alexander sometimes, and Connie Kay. Benny went through drummers like — going out of style.
about_quotes.doc_title: George Masso interviewed by Monk Rowe, Aspen, Colorado, October 10, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Jones Hank
about_quotes.evidence: Mousey Alexander, drummer... At the time that I was with him.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jones Hank — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
